ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for stir-fried straw mushrooms with pea shoots. Mapped to the closest parent class 'stir-fried food product' (FOODON_03315671) as an approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (mushrooms and other fungi, cooked) used as the closest defensible anchor. No specific code exists for the combined straw mushroom and pea shoot stir-fry preparation.

main_ingredients: light soy sauce or oyster sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of stir-fried straw mushrooms with pea shoots is approximately 150 g, representing a typical side-dish portion at a Cantonese meal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Mushrooms, straw, canned, drained solids (FDC ID 169401) and Pea shoots, raw (FDC ID 2346392), combined with cooking-oil addition estimate
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for the combined stir-fried dish. Values are adapted from USDA FDC entries for straw mushrooms (FDC ID 169401) and pea shoots / pea greens (FDC ID 2346392), blended in an approximate 45:45 ratio with a 10% allowance for cooking oil (vegetable oil, FDC ID 172336) and light soy sauce sodium contribution. Energy, macros, and micronutrients are cross-checked against the input AI-generated values; where USDA-derived estimates align within ±15% of the input values, the input values are retained. Sodium is adjusted upward to reflect soy sauce addition. This is an estimated composite record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific stir-fried straw mushroom and pea shoot dish. GI estimated at 15 (low) by analogy with cooked non-starchy vegetables and edible fungi, which consistently register GI values of 10–20 in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: (15 × 4.5 g available carbohydrate per 100 g × 1.5) / 100 = 1.0 g, confirming negligible glycemic impact.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium from soy sauce; high potassium content
hypertension.guidance: At 180 mg sodium per 100 g (270 mg per 150 g serving), this dish contributes a moderate sodium load; individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and benefit from the dish's high potassium content (280 mg/100 g), which supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (estimated 15) and low available carbohydrate (4.5 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 15 and a glycemic load of only 1.0 per 150 g serving, this dish has negligible impact on postprandial blood glucose and is well-suited for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; the 2.5 g dietary fibre per 100 g further slows glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, cholesterol-free, high potassium and antioxidant vitamins
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is cholesterol-free with only 0.6 g saturated fat per 100 g; its high potassium (280 mg), vitamin C (15 mg), and vitamin K (150 mcg) content, combined with anti-inflammatory polysaccharides from straw mushrooms, support cardiovascular health when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory antioxidants from pea shoots and mushroom polysaccharides
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pea shoots provide vitamin C (15 mg/100 g) and folate (45 mcg/100 g), while straw mushrooms contribute beta-glucan polysaccharides with documented anti-inflammatory properties, making this dish a suitable choice for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-Fried Straw Mushrooms with Pea Shoots (草菇炒豆苗, Cǎo Gū Chǎo Dòu Miáo) is a classic Cantonese side dish that showcases the technique of 'wok hei' — the smoky, complex aroma imparted by rapid cooking over extremely high heat in a seasoned carbon steel wok. The dish combines tender, egg-shaped straw mushrooms (Volvariella volvacea) with delicate young pea shoots (dou miao), seasoned minimally with garlic, ginger, and a splash of light soy sauce or oyster sauce to preserve the natural sweetness and freshness of the vegetables. The result is a crisp-tender texture with a vibrant green colour and a clean, slightly earthy flavour profile. Nutritionally, the dish is low in calories (65 kcal/100 g), low in carbohydrates (4.5 g/100 g), and provides a meaningful amount of dietary fibre (2.5 g/100 g), vitamin A (120 mcg RAE/100 g), vitamin K (150 mcg/100 g), folate (45 mcg/100 g), and potassium (280 mg/100 g). Its estimated glycemic index of 15 makes it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The moderate sodium content (180 mg/100 g) from soy sauce warrants attention for those with hypertension, though the high potassium content partially offsets this concern. The dish is vegetarian and dairy-free, and is commonly served as a side dish at Cantonese family meals, dim sum banquets, and healthy weeknight dinners throughout Southern China, Hong Kong, and Chinese diaspora communities in Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific combined stir-fried dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for straw mushrooms and pea shoots with a cooking-oil and soy-sauce sodium adjustment. The input AI-generated values are broadly consistent with USDA component data and have been retained where within ±15% tolerance. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for cooked non-starchy vegetables. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: this is a plant-based dish containing no animal products; vitamin B12 is naturally zero in this food. trans_fat_g is 0.0: no hydrogenated oils are used in traditional preparation; confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central vegetable oil entries. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: the dish is entirely plant-based and cholesterol-free, naturally zero. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this specific stir-fried composite dish is not reported in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source consulted as of 2026-04-17. 3. Vitamin K is reported at 150 mcg/100 g, primarily from pea shoots; individuals on warfarin or other vitamin K-sensitive anticoagulant therapy should be advised to consume this dish consistently rather than avoiding it, and to consult their clinician. 4. The dish is noted as 'vegan' in the input dietary_tags; however, oyster sauce (a common ingredient) is not vegan. The is_vegan flag has been set to false to reflect the typical preparation; a vegan variant using light soy sauce only is possible. 5. Saturated fat (0.6 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (2.1 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.7 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central vegetable oil (FDC ID 172336) contribution at approximately 5 g oil per 100 g cooked dish, consistent with the total fat value of 4.8 g/100 g. 6. Duplicate portion entry in input (two identical 150 g entries) has been deduplicated to a single 150 g default serving.
